modern_denom_mapping: Eastern Orthodox (Antioch); Syriac Orthodox; Maronite; Catholic
evidence_notes_and_citations: Summary: Antioch is an early episcopal center; Ignatius’ letters presuppose structured church life in multiple named cities, including his own see. Uncertainty: apostolic founding traditions vary by later sources. Evidence: Ignatian corpus (early 2nd c). Citations: https://www.newadvent.org/fathers/0109.htm

Summary: Christians are attested province-wide in Bithynia-Pontus in Pliny’s correspondence with Trajan (c. 111–112); Nicomedia is a major city in that province, so presence is probable even if the letter is not city-specific. Uncertainty: letter does not name Nicomedia directly. Evidence: Pliny, Ep. 10.96–97. Citations: https://faculty.georgetown.edu/jod/texts/pliny.html

Summary: North African Christianity is securely attested by the Acts of the Scillitan Martyrs (trial held at Carthage, 180). By the early 3rd century, Carthage is also tied to Tertullian, later associated with Montanism. Uncertainty: "Montanist" is a movement label; local adherence varies by person/group. Evidence: Scillitan martyr record; reference biography of Tertullian. Citations: https://sourcebooks.web.fordham.edu/source/scillitan-mart.asp ; https://www.britannica.com/biography/Tertullian

key_figures: Constantine I; Arius; Hosius of Corduba
evidence_notes_and_citations: Summary: Nicaea hosts the first ecumenical council in 325 under Constantine, convened to address the Arian controversy; this implies an established episcopal network and ecclesial infrastructure. Uncertainty: exact local Christian community development prior to the council varies by source. Evidence: standard council chronologies. Citations: https://www.britannica.com/event/First-Council-of-Nicaea-325 ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First_Council_of_Nicaea

Summary: Aksumite coinage shows a shift to overtly Christian symbols after Ezana’s conversion (often dated c. 340–356), supporting mid-4th-century institutional Christianity. Uncertainty: precise conversion year and sequence of changes are debated. Evidence: numismatic/curatorial synthesis. Citations: https://smarthistory.org/aksumite-coins/
